codeReview.summary: The backend commands cover the core gh/gist flows and error mapping, but the reviewed commit does not fully satisfy the required configurable machine-name behavior and lacks tests for the gh-facing command paths and failure handling the feature description explicitly calls out.

issues.description: `sync_beacon_push` derives the machine name exclusively from `AppConfig.computer_name`/environment via `machine_name_from_config()`. The feature and expected behavior require the Sync Beacon machine name to be configurable via the dedicated sync-beacon setting, but this commit ignores `sync_beacon_machine_name`, so custom beacon names from settings are never published until the later fix feature.

The implementation has no command-level tests covering the gh-backed behaviors promised by the feature description (missing/unauthenticated gh, gist 404/access denial, network failure classification, create vs update flows, config persistence after gist creation). The only new tests exercise pure JSON/helper functions, so the main risk surface of the feature remains unverified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `tauri-fullstack` skill did not help the worker validate backend command integrations that shell out to external CLIs. The worker deviated from test-first and shipped a backend-only gh integration with only helper/unit coverage, missing the configurable machine-name requirement that the later fix feature had to repair.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill `/.factory/skills/tauri-fullstack/SKILL.md` only prescribes generic Rust tests, while the worker handoff for `sync-beacon-backend` says CLI/network branches were only manually inspected and not integration-tested; the reviewed commit also had to be followed by `fix-beacon-machine-name-and-commit-metadata` (handoff commit `7ddc406`) to correct machine-name behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture docs mention GitHub Gist usage but do not record the important distinction between the generic `computerName` setting and the dedicated `syncBeaconMachineName` value used by the Sync Beacon flows, which likely contributed to the backend using the wrong field initially.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission `AGENTS.md` documents the gist commands but not the beacon-specific machine-name source, and the reviewed commit's `machine_name_from_config()` used `computer_name` until the later fix updated it to prefer `sync_beacon_machine_name`.

summary: Reviewed `sync-beacon-backend` at commit `2c79d8f`. The gh/gist backend foundation is solid, but the feature fails review because it published the wrong machine-name source and lacked verification for the command/error-handling paths central to the feature.
